title: [lichen_l2.c:1004-1006] tx_mutex re-acquire impossible after enable fails
description:
What: In lichen_l2_enable()'s enable path, if start() fails and link_ctx was initialized (link_ctx_initialized_here == true), the rollback code at lines 1079-1086 re-acquires both mutexes. However, tx_mutex and rx_mutex were already released at lines 1050-1051.

Why it matters: This is NOT a bug. The code correctly re-acquires the mutexes for the rollback. I was mistaken. The flow is:
1. Lines 1004-1005: Lock both mutexes
2. Lines 1006-1048: Initialize link_ctx if needed
3. Lines 1050-1051: Unlock both mutexes
4. Line 1059: start() called
5. If start() fails and link_ctx_initialized_here:
6. Lines 1080-1081: Re-lock both mutexes (this is correct)
7. Line 1082-1083: Reset link_ctx
8. Lines 1084-1085: Unlock both mutexes

This is actually correct. Closing.
close_reason: Not a bug - code correctly re-acquires mutexes after releasing them for the rollback path.
